revision_notes: Revised after direct viewing of 8 key photos from Drive. Specific filenames now assigned to sequences 1, 2, and 4 where confirmed photos are strong. Unviewed photos referenced by filename with architect review requests. Sequence 3 (YouTube) remains contingent — no video confirmed.

posts.post_type: anchor — single image or carousel (rain first, sun second)
posts.cover_asset: _DSC1551.JPG — CONFIRMED VIEWED. Low-angle dry golden-brown scrub against grey overcast sky, horizon line cutting the frame in half. Rain day, January 2023. Expose for the sky. Do not correct toward warmth. This is the anchor cover.
asset_order: _DSC1551.JPG — FRAME 1 (COVER): rain-condition horizon frame. Grey overcast sky, dry scrub, horizon as dividing line. No building, no architectural element. The grey-blue luminosity is the image. Confirmed viewed — strong anchor candidate.

_DSC2062.JPG — FRAME 2 (SUN): CONFIRMED VIEWED. Construction stakes in ground, Pacific as blue strip on right horizon, mountains left, sky dominant. October 2023. NOTE: this frame introduces the stakes (construction has begun) — it reads as continuation of the same thesis in different weather and season. If the carousel logic requires a frame with no built element at all, substitute a sun-day horizon frame from subfolder 2B (not yet confirmed — see flags). If the stakes read as landscape rather than architecture at this scale, this frame works. Ileana to confirm on final review.

_DSC2083.JPG — OPTIONAL FRAME 3: CONFIRMED VIEWED. Sand dune, tall coastal scrub lit by warm late sun, Pacific barely visible left as dark-blue strip, sky pale blue. February 2023. Use only if carousel runs to three frames and a closer vegetation-scale frame is needed between the wide horizon and the sun anchor. Do not use as cover.
posts.framing_notes: Landscape orientation for all frames. The horizon line must sit in the upper half of the frame — do not center it; let the sea carry the weight of the lower two-thirds so the dissolving upper edge reads as the subject, not the boundary. No architectural element, no scrub silhouetted against sky unless it reads as landscape texture rather than built edge. For the rain frame (cover): expose for the sky, not the sand — the grey-blue luminosity of the overcast Pacific is the image. Do not correct the color toward warmth. Do not sharpen or add contrast. If the frame contains a figure, it must be incidental and small — scale only, never mood staging. For the sun frame: same compositional constraint. Do not select the most saturated image in the folder. The thesis is a condition, not a destination — avoid any frame that reads as aspirational coastal photography. Both frames must be cropped identically or near-identically so the carousel transition from rain to sun is a single sustained look at the same threshold, not two different photographs of two different places.
flags: CONFIRMED: _DSC1551.JPG is viewed and approved as the rain-condition cover asset for this anchor. No further architect confirmation required for this selection.

CONDITIONAL: _DSC2062.JPG (construction stakes, October 2023) is confirmed viewed and strong. It is proposed as the sun-condition second frame. However, the stakes may introduce a built element that conflicts with the 'no architectural element' instruction. Ileana must decide on final review whether the stakes read as landscape-scale markers or as built elements. If stakes are disqualifying, this frame must be replaced.

UNCONFIRMED SUN-DAY FALLBACK: If _DSC2062.JPG is disqualified for the second frame, the ideal replacement is a sun-day wide horizon frame from subfolder 2B (sun day, Feb 2023) — but subfolder 2B has no confirmed drive ID. Architects must confirm subfolder 2B access and pull a candidate before the post is scheduled if _DSC2062.JPG is rejected.
Do not post this anchor until both frames have been reviewed by Ileana and Diego together and the caption copy is confirmed final. Hold the post rather than publish a placeholder.

posts.post_type: process post — site visit documentation, grid carousel
posts.cover_asset: _DSC1551.JPG is already used as the sequence 1 anchor cover — do not repeat. Cover for sequence 2 should be a site-edge frame distinct from the anchor: _DSC2083.JPG (sand dune, coastal scrub, Pacific left) is the preferred opening frame IF it was not used in sequence 1 carousel. If _DSC2083.JPG was used in sequence 1, the cover falls to an unviewed frame from subfolder 1B — architects must confirm a candidate (see flags).
asset_order: _DSC2083.JPG — FRAME 1 (COVER, conditional): CONFIRMED VIEWED. Sand dune, tall coastal scrub lit warm, Pacific barely visible left. This is the site edge — the threshold between scrub, sand, and sea. Use only if not already used in sequence 1 carousel. Strong opening for a site-reading post.

_DSC1551.JPG — DO NOT REPEAT if used as sequence 1 cover. If sequence 1 ran as a single rain image (not carousel), then _DSC1551.JPG may appear here as frame 2, carrying the rain condition into the process narrative.

_DSC1580.JPG — FRAME 2 or 3: CONFIRMED VIEWED. Hand holding small yellow flower found on site, sand and dry vegetation in background. Intimate, ground-level. This is the moment of close attention — the team reading the site at the scale of what grows there. Moves the carousel from landscape to detail. January 2023.

_DSC1697.JPG — FRAME 3 or 4: CONFIRMED VIEWED. Native coastal scrub close-up, green rounded leaves with small red berries, backlit. Site ecology at intimate scale. Continues the close-reading movement of the carousel — what the site holds, not what it looks like from distance. January 2023.

_DSC1849.JPG — FRAME 4 or 5: CONFIRMED VIEWED. Site looking inland, Sierra de la Laguna mountains on horizon, blue sky. This reverses the direction of the gaze — the house will live between two horizons, ocean behind and mountains ahead. Use as a closing frame to complete the spatial orientation of the site-reading sequence. February 2023.

UNVIEWED CANDIDATES from subfolder 1B (rain documentation) — if the carousel needs 1–2 additional frames showing the team at the site edge in rain condition, architects should open subfolder 1B (drive ID: 149gTqaZPgKA63e4FhpkdglUn0mVw3Wtw) and identify frames from: _DSC1556.JPG, _DSC1573.JPG, _DSC1585.JPG, _DSC1651.JPG, _DSC1657.JPG, _DSC1683.JPG. ARCHITECT REQUEST: Pull 2–3 candidates from this set that show the scrub-sand-sea threshold or the team reading the site (figures small, facing away). Ileana must review before confirming any unviewed frame.
posts.framing_notes: This carousel documents the act of reading a site, not the beauty of the site. The opening frame should feel like arriving: landscape scale, the edge legible, weather present. Subsequent frames move progressively closer — from the wide site edge to the detail of the ground, the texture of the scrub, the color of the sand under rain. The final frame (if a sun-day image is included) should be the same compositional view as the rain frames — same horizon line position, same landscape proportions — so the series reads as sustained observation of one place over time, not a tour of picturesque moments. Do not select any frame that foregrounds a tripod, camera bag, or equipment. Do not select any frame that shows the team posing or looking at the camera. If figures appear, they must be small, facing away, incidental to the landscape's scale. No color correction toward warmth. No sharpening of horizon detail.
flags: CONFIRMED VIEWED: _DSC2083.JPG, _DSC1580.JPG, _DSC1697.JPG, _DSC1849.JPG are all confirmed viewed and strong for this carousel. Asset order above is a proposal — Ileana confirms final sequence after reviewing in-layout.

CONDITIONAL COVER: _DSC2083.JPG is the preferred cover only if not used in sequence 1. The two carousels must not open with the same frame. Confirm sequence 1 final asset list before locking this cover.

UNVIEWED RAIN FRAMES NEEDED: The carousel should include at least one frame from the rain documentation that shows the site edge in grey/overcast condition, not just the sunny close-up details. _DSC1556.JPG, _DSC1573.JPG, _DSC1585.JPG, _DSC1651.JPG, _DSC1657.JPG, _DSC1683.JPG are all January 2023, unviewed. ARCHITECT REQUEST: Open subfolder 1B (drive ID: 149gTqaZPgKA63e4FhpkdglUn0mVw3Wtw) and identify 2–3 frames from this set showing the site edge or team-at-site in rain condition.

Rain images in this post are required, not optional. If the unviewed subfolder 1B review yields fewer than 2 strong frames meeting the above criteria, flag immediately — the post should not proceed with only sun-day or detail photography.
Subfolder 2B (sun day, Feb 2023) has no confirmed drive ID. If a sun-day closing frame is needed for this carousel as a final beat, architects must confirm subfolder 2B access.

posts.post_type: literary reference post — single image, grid
posts.cover_asset: _DSC1792.JPG — CONFIRMED VIEWED. Coastal scrub heavily backlit against dark background, single tiny purple flower on a pale branch. Very moody, dark, contemplative. Strong literary register. February 2023. This is the image for the Williams or Solnit register — the bodily need to look, the blue of distance. It earns its stillness.
asset_order: _DSC1792.JPG — SINGLE IMAGE: CONFIRMED VIEWED. Backlit scrub, single purple flower, dark moody background. Literary register is precise — this is not a site survey image, it is an image of attention. Of standing still for a long time before pressing the shutter. Do not crop. Do not correct toward warmth. Do not sharpen. The darkness is the image. February 2023.
posts.framing_notes: Single image post. The image carries the full weight of the copy's bodily register — the need to look outward, the direction the body remembers. _DSC1792.JPG is confirmed as a strong candidate: it is close, intimate, and moody — noticeably different in composition from the wide-horizon anchor (sequence 1) and from the ground-level detail frames of the process carousel (sequence 2). This image should feel close to the site's physical particulars rather than surveying from distance. The dark background and backlit subject place the viewer inside the vegetation rather than outside looking at it — the body is already at the edge. No figure needed. The color palette remains consistent: no warm correction, no contrast boost. The moody darkness is found, not constructed.
flags: CONFIRMED: _DSC1792.JPG is viewed and approved as the primary candidate for this sequence. No further architect confirmation required for this selection.

COPY DEPENDENCY: Diego must decide whether the anchor post (sequence 1) leaned into the Solnit distance register or the Williams bodily-need register before Ileana confirms _DSC1792.JPG as the final image for this post. The image can carry either register — but the copy written for this post must be resolved first. If the anchor opened with Williams ('the need to look'), then Solnit's 'blue of distance' belongs here and the image works. If the anchor opened with Solnit's distance register, then Williams' bodily need is the calibration here and the image still works. The image is not register-contingent — but confirm copy direction before scheduling.
DO NOT USE: _DSC1792.JPG must not appear in sequences 1 or 2 carousel. It is reserved for this post. Confirm it has not been pulled for any prior use.

ALTERNATIVE if _DSC1792.JPG is disqualified for any reason: _DSC1501.JPG (dry golden grasses close-up, ground level, sun day, August 2023) is a potential fallback — confirmed viewed, intimate scale, ground-level attention — but its sun-day palette and golden warmth are a different register from the literary darkness of _DSC1792.JPG. It is a weaker candidate for this post but a viable one. Ileana to confirm if needed.

posts.post_type: thesis-to-architecture carousel — site photo first, section sketch or plan fragment second, render last (conditional)
posts.cover_asset: _DSC2062.JPG is the preferred opening frame for this carousel IF it was not used in sequence 1 (construction stakes, October 2023, horizon present, site before house). If _DSC2062.JPG was used in sequence 1, the opening frame for this carousel should come from subfolders 3–6 (Oct 2023, drive IDs not yet confirmed) or fall back to _DSC1849.JPG (site looking inland, mountains on horizon, blue sky) if not used in sequence 2.
asset_order: _DSC2062.JPG — FRAME 1 (conditional, preferred): CONFIRMED VIEWED. Construction stakes in ground, Pacific on right horizon, mountains left, golden scrub, sky dominant. October 2023. This frame shows the thesis becoming architectural action — the stakes mark the beginning of the house. The horizon is already present as the organizing element. Use only if not already used in sequence 1. If used here, it works as the opening argument: this is what the site gives.

_DSC1849.JPG — FRAME 1 (fallback if _DSC2062.JPG is used in sequence 1): CONFIRMED VIEWED. Site looking inland, mountains on horizon, blue sky. February 2023. Shows the site's double orientation — the horizon not only toward the Pacific but toward the sierra. Use only if not already used in sequence 2.

PLAN OR SECTION SKETCH — FRAME 2: ARCHITECTURAL DRAWING — not confirmed in assets.json. Must be sourced separately from the project drawing archive. ARCHITECT REQUEST: Provide a clean export of the plan and/or section sketch. Minimum 1080px on the short axis. Studio graphic language, no revision notes, no dimension overlays. The horizon axis should be legible in the drawing. Confirm filename and Drive location once exported.

VISTA01_FRONT FACADE.jpg — RENDER FRAME 3A: exterior front facade. Downloaded 2026-03-29. Best thesis candidate — exterior relationship to site and horizon most likely present. Use only if joint quality review (Diego and Ileana) confirms it reads as translation of sensory thesis, not lifestyle visualization.

VISTA08_BACK FACADE.jpg — RENDER FRAME 3B (alternate): exterior rear facade. Downloaded 2026-03-29. Second-best thesis candidate. Review against VISTA01 — choose the one where horizon reads as organizing axis, not as scenery.
VISTA02_CENTRAL PATIO.jpg — RENDER CANDIDATE: central patio. Review for horizon legibility. If patio reads as lifestyle amenity, disqualify.
VISTA03_LIVING.jpg — RENDER CANDIDATE: interior living. Higher risk for lifestyle read — review critically.
VISTA04_DINING.jpg — RENDER CANDIDATE: dining area interior.
VISTA05_MASTER BEDROOM.jpg — RENDER CANDIDATE: master bedroom interior.
VISTA06_POOL.jpg — RENDER: pool. High likelihood of lifestyle/amenity read — use only if pool is clearly secondary to horizon in the composition.
VISTA07_FIREPIT.jpg — RENDER: firepit. Same caution as pool.
RENDERS NOTE: Only one render enters the carousel (last frame). If none passes quality review, carousel runs as site photograph + sketch only.

UNVIEWED CANDIDATES for consideration if stronger Oct 2023 frame needed: _DSC2066.JPG (Oct 2023, not yet viewed — ARCHITECT REQUEST: confirm content. If this shows site with stakes or early construction in a wider or cleaner horizon frame than _DSC2062.JPG, it may be preferred here).
posts.framing_notes: The carousel's visual argument is: this is what the site gives — this is what the architecture receives — this is how it answers. The opening site photograph must show the horizon as a spatial and organizing presence, not as scenery. A frame from Oct 2023 (subfolders 3–6 or root pool Oct frames) may show a later stage of site understanding — preferred if available and not used in sequence 1. The plan or section sketch should be photographed or exported cleanly — not a phone snap of a paper drawing, but a legible architectural document rendered in the studio's graphic language. The render (if included) must be reviewed by Ileana and Diego together before it enters the carousel: it must read as a translation of the thesis into form, not as a photorealistic visualization of a desirable house. If the render foregrounds comfort, pool, terrace, or any lifestyle element, it does not pass. If the render's compositional logic does not rhyme with the site photographs (horizon line position, landscape proportion, palette), it does not pass. If the render passes, it appears last — never first, never before the site photograph. If it does not pass, the carousel runs as site photograph + sketch only, and the copy still lands.
flags: CONFIRMED VIEWS: _DSC2062.JPG and _DSC1849.JPG are both confirmed viewed and available for the opening site photograph of this carousel. The choice between them depends on whether _DSC2062.JPG was used in sequence 1 and whether _DSC1849.JPG was used in sequence 2 — confirm prior sequence asset lists before locking this selection.

ARCHITECTURAL DRAWINGS NOT IN ASSETS: process_images array in assets.json is empty. Sequence 5 requires a plan or section fragment as the second carousel frame. This must be sourced from the project drawing archive — it is not in the confirmed asset pool. This is the single most critical unresolved dependency for this post.

RENDERS DOWNLOADED 2026-03-29: 9 renders confirmed in assets.json (VISTA01–VISTA08 + VISTA041). Files available locally in photos/. Exterior candidates for thesis alignment: VISTA01_FRONT FACADE.jpg and VISTA08_BACK FACADE.jpg. Diego and Ileana must still conduct joint quality review — checking whether any render shows the horizon as organizing axis and avoids lifestyle/amenity emphasis. This review cannot be delegated or done asynchronously from thumbnails.

UNVIEWED CANDIDATE: _DSC2066.JPG is October 2023, not yet viewed. If it shows a wider or cleaner horizon frame than _DSC2062.JPG with the construction stakes, architects should open it for Ileana to review as a potential alternative opening frame.

Subfolders 3, 4, 5, and 6 (Oct 2023) have no confirmed drive IDs — if accessible, an Oct 2023 frame from these subfolders is preferred over the root pool for this post. Architects must provide access if possible.
Do not publish this post until the drawing and render decisions are resolved. This post has the highest asset dependency of any post in the campaign.

render_recommendation: Renders should appear in this campaign only under the following conditions, all of which must be true simultaneously: (1) the render quality review (Diego and Ileana together, full-size files) confirms that at least one render reads as a translation of the sensory thesis into architectural form — horizon as organizing axis, palette consistent with the site photography, no lifestyle or amenity emphasis; (2) the render appears as the third and final frame in the sequence 5 carousel, after the site photograph and after the plan or section sketch — never as a standalone image, never as the lead frame, never in any other sequence position; (3) the render does not appear on the website page until it has been used in sequence 5 — the website receives the campaign's visual logic, it does not debut new material. If these conditions cannot all be confirmed before sequence 5's publish date of 2026-03-31, the render is omitted from phase 1 entirely. The campaign's argument is complete without the render — sequence 5's copy works from site photograph to sketch alone. The render is an addition to a complete argument, not a required element.
